title: carduus × orthocephalus nothosubsp. carniolicus
intro: Carduus × orthocephalus nothosubsp. carniolicus, also known as the Carniolan thistle or Alpine thistle, is a rare and endemic subspecies of thistle found in the alpine regions of Slovenia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are believed to help reduce swelling and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Carduus × orthocephalus nothosubsp. carniolicus are thought to include improving digestion, reducing fever, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
